primary_false_move: queue/dispatch treated as just local scheduler law

expected_resolution: keep queue mutation and dispatch receipts downstream of session authority

primary_false_move: runtime reporting treated as authority because it sees state
required_boundary: runtime/session authority owns session identity and persistence

primary_false_move: api entry treated as whole center because it is operationally visible
required_boundary: api entry binds carrier into center but does not replace it
expected_resolution: keep api entry downstream of runtime/session authority

primary_false_move: queue or api entry silently answers enactment permission
required_boundary: activation law remains outside Lane C center

primary_false_move: continuation bundle treated as whole session identity
required_boundary: continuation preserves thread identity but not full session authority

primary_false_move: settlement result treated as authority source
required_boundary: settlement classifies closure rather than creating runtime/session authority
expected_resolution: keep closure logic downstream of lawful session path
